body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_6)
Regime data: VIX 17.22 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | BULLISH conf 53.333333333333336% | Range 728.65–768.57 | put_credit_spread x0
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows elevated volatility with VIX at 17.22, a steep contango between 9-day (15.15) and 3-month (19.73) implied vols, and a high HY OAS at 269 bps, signaling persistent risk aversion amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions that are dragging Nasdaq-leading stocks and SPY lower to $748.61 with a negative sentiment of -1. NVDA at $203.81 and AMZN at $247.27 stand out with strong positive sentiment (+1) and elevated IVs (50.45% and 46.67%, respectively), reflecting a generational transfer narrative favoring semiconductor leaders like NVIDIA and undervalued tech giants like Amazon, which is at decade-low prices. The key near-term catalyst is the upcoming bank earnings next week, starting with JPM, BAC, WFC, and GS on July 14, which could either confirm financial sector resilience or exacerbate market jitters given the hawkish Fed commentary and sticky inflation risks. The biggest risk is escalating geopolitical tensions with Iran, which have already pressured tech stocks and could trigger broader risk-off flows if the conflict intensifies, compounded by the threat of a Fed rate hike if inflation surprises to the upside this week. Trade signal gates are fully active, signaling readiness to capitalize on directional moves amid heightened uncertainty.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq leading stocks lower as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate

NVDA.sentiment_driver: NVIDIA and Micron the Winners in generational transfer
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon stock hasn't been this cheap in over a decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, BAC, WFC, and GS reporting on July 14 and MS following on July 15. Investors will focus on credit quality and loan growth amid a still elevated HY OAS of 269 bps and VIX at 17.22 in contango, which suggests cautious risk sentiment. The financial sector’s performance will set the tone for SPY, with muted expectations limiting upside for cyclical names like NVDA and AMZN in the near term.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key industrial and communication services earnings, notably TSLA and T on July 22, alongside materials and real estate reports. TSLA’s bullish outlook could provide a positive catalyst for consumer discretionary and tech sectors, potentially lifting NVDA and AMZN, while industrials like HON and RTX will offer insight into manufacturing demand amid mixed macro signals. The absence of major macro events keeps focus on earnings-driven sector rotation.
narratives.next_month: The following month brings a heavy slate including the July 30 FOMC meeting, with a 65% chance of a rate hold at 3.50%, and the July 31 core PCE inflation print, which carries bearish risks if above 0.2%. Earnings from AMZN, CVX, and XOM will be critical for gauging consumer and energy sector resilience as inflation data could pressure discretionary spending and capex. AMD’s cautious outlook on AI accelerator shipments also tempers enthusiasm for tech growth, potentially weighing on NVDA and broader market sentiment.
